subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Dark Nebulae: Cosmic Shadows
description: A quick, practical guide to Dark Nebulae: Cosmic Shadows.

hook.text: Dark nebulae are dense clouds of dust that block the light of stars behind them, creating silhouettes against bright backgrounds.

buildup.text: They contain tiny grains of carbon and silicates. These dust particles absorb and scatter visible light almost completely.

discovery.text: The Horsehead Nebula is the most iconic dark nebula—a pillar of dust shaped like a chess knight against a red emission backdrop.

twist.text: Dark nebulae are often the densest parts of molecular clouds—the very regions where new stars are about to form.

climax.text: Infrared telescopes can see through the dust. JWST revealed hundreds of young stars hidden inside the Horsehead.

punchline.text: Shadows in the sky. Inside them, the next generation of stars is being born.

quiz.question: What makes dark nebulae appear dark?
options: Dense dust grains block the light from stars and nebulae behind them
They absorb all energy and convert it to gravitational waves
They are voids in space with no matter or radiation at all
